lesson.menu_desc: Review + David's full visit day combining imperatives / directions, small talk questions, would like / prefer and opinions from lessons 16-19 -- 30 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- Checkpoint 3 -- David's Day in São Paulo
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maísa de Oliveira Santos | Aula 20 -- Checkpoint 3

listenings.text: Hi Maisa, this is David. Thank you for a wonderful day at your office in Sao Paulo. In the morning, you welcomed me. You said come in and have a seat. Then we had some small talk. The weather was warm and sunny, and we talked about my weekend. At lunch, we went to a nice restaurant. The waiter brought the menu, and I was ready to order. I would like the grilled fish, and it was delicious. In the afternoon, we had a meeting. I think our new plan is good, and I agree with the team about the deadline. It was a great day. See you tomorrow. Thank you!

Hello, this is Sarah from the office. Let me tell you about our visitor day. In the morning, I welcomed our guest at the reception. I said welcome, please come in, and have a seat. Then we had a little small talk about the weather and the weekend. At noon, we went to lunch. The waiter brought the menu, and I was ready to order. I would like a green salad, please. In the afternoon, we had a short meeting. I think the new plan is good, but in my opinion the budget is a little high. I agree with David about the deadline. It was a busy and happy day. Have a nice day!

extra_audio.text: Welcome, David. Please come in and have a seat. How was your weekend? The weather is beautiful today. Here is the menu. What would you like to order? In the meeting, I think our plan is good.
